name: Algonquin
text: algonquin ingredients 2 oz rye 1 oz pineapple juice ¼ oz grenadine steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice and shake. strain into a coupe. notes the classic algonquin doesn't use grenadine, but many bartenders add just a touch — this is that variation.

name: Amanda's Famous Chili
text: amanda's famous chili ingredients 1¼ lbs ground beef 2 cups diced onion 1 cup diced celery 1 can diced tomatoes 2½ tbsp chili powder 2½ tsp cumin 1½ tsp salt 1¼ cups water ½ tsp garlic powder 1 bay leaf 1 tsp oregano ¼ tsp cayenne pepper 1 can kidney beans, drained 1 can chili beans, undrained steps sauté the meat with the onions and celery over medium heat until the meat loses its pink color. add all of the remaining ingredients except the beans. simmer, stirring occasionally, for 1 hour. add the beans and simmer the mixture for 15 minutes longer. remove the bay leaf before serving. serve with shredded cheese, diced onions, and sour cream. notes diced tomatoes: i usually blend them up so there are no chunks. the chili will hold, refrigerated, for 4 days.

name: Amaretto Sour
text: amaretto sour ingredients 1½ oz amaretto ¾ oz cask-proof bourbon 1 oz lemon juice 1 tsp rich simple syrup (2:1) ½ oz egg white lemon peel and cherries, for garnish steps add all ingredients to a shaker and dry shake (no ice). add ice and shake again, then strain over fresh ice. garnish with a lemon peel and brandied cherries. notes rating: b. source liquor.com — amaretto sour

name: American Trilogy
text: american trilogy ingredients 1 oz rye 1 oz apple brandy ¼ oz demerara syrup 2 dashes orange bitters orange peel, for garnish steps stir with ice. serve over a big ice cube. garnish with an expressed orange peel. source robb report — the best american trilogy recipe

name: Apple Cider Old Fashioned
text: apple cider old fashioned ingredients 2 oz bourbon 1 oz apple cider ¼ oz maple syrup apple slice or cinnamon stick, for garnish steps add the bourbon, apple cider, and maple syrup to a rocks glass with ice. stir to chill. garnish with an apple slice or a cinnamon stick. notes amounts are a starting point — adjust the cider and maple to taste. source foxes love lemons — apple cider old fashioned

name: Apple Jack
text: apple jack ingredients 2 oz apple brandy ¾ oz lemon juice ¾ oz simple syrup 1 oz apple cider steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice. shake, then strain. notes smaller-batch ratio also works: 1½ oz apple brandy, ½ oz lemon, ½ oz simple syrup, ½ oz apple cider.

name: Benton's Bacon Old Fashioned
text: benton's bacon old fashioned ingredients 2 oz bacon-infused bourbon (see below) ¼ oz maple syrup 2–3 dashes angostura bitters orange peel, for garnish steps add the bacon-infused bourbon, maple syrup, and bitters to a glass with ice. stir. express an orange peel over the drink and drop it in as garnish. bacon-infused bourbon 8 oz bourbon 1 tbsp non-scorched bacon fat (liquid) combine and let sit at room temperature for ~7 hours. freeze ~2 hours. filter until clear. notes freaking awesome! bourbons tried: early times was the best — better than kirkland 120 proof, and better than sazerac. to infuse a full 750 ml bottle (≈25.36 oz), use 3+ tbsp bacon fat.

name: Better Than Sex Cake
text: better than sex cake ingredients 1 box cake mix 3 eggs ¾ cup water ¾ cup oil 8 oz sour cream or yogurt 6 oz mini chocolate chips steps mix well and pour into a greased bundt pan. bake at 350°f for 50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. sprinkle with powdered sugar, or pour the glaze on top. glaze 1 cup powdered sugar a little milk or cream a little melted butter mix together and pour over the cake.

name: Bourbon Paloma
text: bourbon paloma ingredients 2 oz bourbon 4½ oz fever-tree grapefruit soda steps build in a highball glass over ice. notes meh — try with fresh grapefruit and soda instead of the premade soda.

name: Bourbon Sunrise
text: bourbon sunrise ingredients 2 oz bourbon ¾ oz lemon juice ½ oz grenadine splash of soda steps add the bourbon, lemon juice, and grenadine to a glass with ice. top with a splash of soda. notes not the tequila sunrise. very refreshing — a pool drink.

name: Buffalo Chicken Dip
text: buffalo chicken dip ingredients 1 block (8 oz) cream cheese, softened ½ cup frank's redhot sauce ½ cup blue cheese or ranch dressing 1 cup mozzarella cheese, shredded 1–2 cups cooked chicken, chopped or shredded steps mix all ingredients together. bake in a 350°f oven for 30 minutes, or until bubbly around the edges. notes chicken: usually 1 large chicken breast, 2 smaller ones, or whatever leftover cooked chicken is around.

name: Clover Club
text: clover club ingredients 2 oz gin ¾ oz lemon juice ¾ oz simple syrup (or raspberry syrup) 3–5 raspberries 1 egg white lemon peel and raspberries, for garnish steps muddle the raspberries with the syrup in a shaker. add the gin, lemon juice, and egg white; dry shake (no ice). add ice, shake again, and double-strain into a coupe. express a lemon peel over the foam and garnish with raspberries. notes basically a gin sour. rating: a. source liquor.com — clover club robb report — the best clover club recipe

name: Enzoni
text: enzoni ingredients 1 oz gin 1 oz campari ¾ oz lemon juice ½ oz simple syrup 5–6 green grapes, plus more for garnish small pinch of salt (optional) orange slice (optional), for garnish steps add the grapes to a shaker and briefly muddle — break the skins and press the fruit, don't pulverize it into jelly. add the liquids and ice, then shake hard for six to eight seconds. strain over fresh ice in a rocks glass. garnish with more grapes on a pick and, if you like, an orange slice.

name: Filibuster
text: filibuster ingredients 1½–2 oz rye ½ oz lemon juice ½ oz maple syrup 1 egg white angostura bitters and a lemon peel, for garnish steps add the rye, lemon, maple syrup, and egg white to a shaker and dry shake (no ice). add ice and shake again, then strain. garnish with angostura on top and an expressed lemon peel. notes a rye sour with maple syrup.

name: Fitzgerald
text: fitzgerald ingredients 2 oz gin ¾ oz lemon juice ¾ oz simple syrup 2 dashes angostura bitters lemon peel, for garnish steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice and shake. strain into a coupe and garnish with a lemon peel. notes meh.

name: Fruit Bread or Muffins
text: fruit bread or muffins ingredients ½ cup soft butter 1 cup sugar 2 tsp baking soda 2 tsp baking powder 1 tsp salt 2 eggs 2 tsp vanilla 16 oz yogurt ¼ cup milk 4 cups fruit 4 cups flour steps cream the butter and sugar together. add the baking soda, baking powder, and salt. add the egg, vanilla, and yogurt; mix well. add the milk. add half of the flour and half of the fruit. add the remaining flour and end with the last of the fruit. sprinkle the top generously with granulated sugar. bake muffins at 400°f for ~30 minutes, or a loaf at 350°f for ~30 minutes. notes bake times are approximate (marked uncertain in the original) — check for doneness.

name: Godfather
text: godfather ingredients 2¼ oz whiskey (scotch, irish, or bourbon) ½ oz amaretto 2 dashes bitters 1 large ice cube orange peel, expressed steps add the whiskey, amaretto, and bitters to a rocks glass. add the large ice cube and stir to chill. express the orange peel over the drink.

name: Gold Rush
text: gold rush ingredients 2 oz bourbon ¾ oz lemon juice ¾ oz honey syrup grapefruit peel, for garnish steps add the bourbon, lemon, honey syrup, and grapefruit peel to a shaker with ice. shake, then strain over fresh ice.

name: Green Beast
text: green beast ingredients 1 oz absinthe (pernod) 1 oz lime juice 1 oz simple syrup 4 oz water 4 cucumber slices, plus more for garnish steps add all ingredients to a pitcher or glass with ice. stir, and garnish with cucumber slices. source liquor.com — green beast

name: Highball
text: highball ingredients 2 oz whiskey 4 oz soda water ice steps fill a glass with ice. add the whiskey, then top with soda. notes rating: b. penelope wheated with a splash of lemon is good. suntory toki is good.

name: Horsefeather
text: horsefeather ingredients 2 oz rye ½ oz lemon juice ¼ oz simple syrup 3 oz ginger beer 2 dashes angostura bitters steps build in a glass with ice. stir.

name: Hot Buttered Rum
text: hot buttered rum ingredients 2 oz aged rum 1 tbsp butter 2 tsp brown sugar pinch ground cinnamon half-pinch ground cloves 6 oz hot water cinnamon stick or grated nutmeg, for garnish steps pre-warm the mug: pour in hot water, wait 30 seconds, then pour it out. add the butter, rum, brown sugar, and spices to the mug, then pour hot water over. stir well to combine. garnish with a cinnamon stick or grated fresh nutmeg (or both). serve with a long spoon or straw for re-mixing.

name: Jack Rose
text: jack rose ingredients 2 oz apple brandy ½ oz lime juice ¾ oz grenadine (or less) steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice and shake. strain into a glass. (shaking is preferred, but building in a rocks glass over ice works similarly.) source robb report — the best jack rose recipe

name: Jalapeño Old Fashioned
text: jalapeño old fashioned ingredients 2 oz bourbon ¼ oz jalapeño syrup 2–3 dashes angostura bitters steps add all ingredients to a glass with ice. stir.

name: Jalapeño Peach Bourbon
text: jalapeño peach bourbon ingredients 4 oz bourbon 1 peach 2 oz jalapeño syrup peach slice and jalapeño slice, for garnish steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice and shake. strain thin onto fresh ice. garnish with a peach slice and a jalapeño slice. notes rating: c. not as good as hoped — a splash of pineapple helped, but still not as good as the pineapple drink.

name: Kentucky 75
text: kentucky 75 ingredients 2 oz rye ½ oz lemon juice ¾ oz simple syrup 2 dashes angostura bitters chilled prosecco, to top steps add the rye, lemon, simple syrup, and bitters to a shaker with ice and shake. strain into a glass and top with chilled prosecco. give a gentle stir.

name: Kentucky Buck
text: kentucky buck ingredients 2 oz bourbon ¾ oz lemon juice ¾ oz ginger syrup 1 strawberry 1–2 dashes angostura bitters about 2 oz soda water half strawberry, lemon wheel, or mint sprig, for garnish steps muddle the strawberry in the bottom of a shaker. add the bourbon, lemon, ginger syrup, and bitters; shake for six to eight seconds. strain over fresh ice in a tall glass and top with soda water. garnish with a half strawberry, a lemon wheel, a mint sprig — or all three.

name: Layered Salad
text: layered salad ingredients 1 head lettuce, shredded 1 medium white onion, thinly sliced 1 can peas, drained bacon bits 1 cup mayo 1 cup sour cream 1 packet dry ranch dressing mix extra-sharp cheddar, shredded steps shred the lettuce into a 9×13 dish. add a layer of thinly sliced onion. add the drained peas. sprinkle on bacon bits. stir together the mayo, sour cream, and ranch packet, then spread over the top. finish with a layer of shredded extra-sharp cheddar.

name: Lemon Muffins
text: lemon muffins ingredients ½ cup soft butter 1 cup sugar 2 tsp baking soda 2 tsp baking powder 1 tsp salt 2 eggs 2 tsp vanilla 16 oz yogurt 2 tsp lemon zest ¼ cup milk 4 cups flour juice from one lemon juice from one orange steps cream the butter and sugar together. add the baking soda, baking powder, and salt. add the egg, vanilla, yogurt, and lemon zest. mix well. add the milk. add half of the flour and half of the juice. add the remaining flour and the rest of the juice. bake at 400°f for ~30 minutes. glaze 1½ cups powdered sugar 2 tbsp lemon juice ¼ tsp almond extract 1 tsp lemon zest whisk together and drizzle over the cooled muffins. notes the original didn't specify a bake temp/time — 400°f for ~30 min is a best guess based on the fruit muffins. adjust as needed.

name: Mac and Cheese
text: mac and cheese ingredients 2 cups+ mueller's elbow pasta 2 lb kraft extra sharp cheddar cheese ½ stick butter ~3 cups milk 1 tbsp flour salt and pepper steps preheat the oven to 325°f. cook the pasta ~10 minutes, then drain. in a baking dish, cube 8 oz of the cheese and the butter. add 1 tbsp flour, plus salt and pepper to taste. add the cooked macaroni and stir to mix. add milk to almost cover the mixture. place cut slices of cheddar on top. bake at 325°f for 1 hour.

name: Manhattan
text: manhattan ingredients 2 oz rye 1 oz sweet vermouth 2 dashes angostura bitters cherry, for garnish steps stir with ice to chill. strain into a glass. garnish with a cherry.

name: Maple Jalapeño Bourbon
text: maple jalapeño bourbon ingredients 2 oz bourbon ½ oz maple syrup 8 half-moon jalapeño slices, plus 5 seeds ¼ lemon, squeezed long jalapeño strips and a lemon slice, for garnish steps muddle the jalapeño slices and seeds with the maple syrup. squeeze in the lemon, add the bourbon, and stir over ice. fine-strain onto fresh ice. garnish with long jalapeño strips and a lemon slice.

name: Mint Julep
text: mint julep ingredients 2 oz bourbon ½ oz simple syrup mint leaves, plus a sprig for garnish steps rub the mint leaves around the inside of the glass. add the bourbon and simple syrup with crushed ice. garnish with a mint sprig. notes use more mint than just the rub. try with jalapeño syrup, or add a splash of club soda.

name: Monte Carlo
text: monte carlo ingredients 2¼ oz rye ½ oz bénédictine 2 dashes angostura bitters lemon peel, for garnish steps stir with ice over a big cube. garnish with a lemon peel.

name: Negroni
text: negroni ingredients 1 oz gin 1 oz campari 1 oz sweet vermouth orange peel, for garnish steps add all ingredients to a glass over a large ice cube. stir until cold. garnish with an orange peel.

name: Paper Plane
text: paper plane ingredients ¾ oz bourbon ¾ oz aperol ¾ oz amaro nonino ¾ oz lemon juice steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice and shake. strain into a coupe. notes equal parts all around. also goes by "paper airplane."

name: Peach Cobbler Dump Cake
text: peach cobbler dump cake probably one of the best "kid week" desserts. ingredients 2 cans sliced peaches in syrup 1 box yellow cake mix 1 stick butter cinnamon (optional) vanilla ice cream, to serve steps dump the peaches (with syrup) into a 9x13 dish. sprinkle the dry cake mix over the top. slice the butter thin and scatter over the cake mix. dust with cinnamon, if using. bake at 350°f for 40–50 minutes, until golden. serve warm with vanilla ice cream.

name: Peanut Butter Old Fashioned
text: peanut butter old fashioned ingredients 1½ oz skrewball peanut butter whiskey 1 oz rye 4 dashes chocolate bitters 1 large ice cube steps add the skrewball, rye, and chocolate bitters to a rocks glass. add the large ice cube and stir to chill.

name: Pineapple Jalapeño Whiskey Sour
text: pineapple jalapeño whiskey sour ingredients 2 oz whiskey 1½ oz pineapple juice ½ oz lemon juice ¾ oz jalapeño syrup jalapeño slice, for garnish steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice and shake. strain onto fresh ice. garnish with a jalapeño slice. notes rating: b.

name: Pink Lady
text: pink lady ingredients 1½ oz gin ½ oz apple brandy ½ oz lemon juice (or lime) ¾ oz grenadine (or less) 1 egg white steps add all ingredients to a shaker and dry shake (no ice). add ice and shake again. strain into a coupe. notes meh — try with a better grenadine. source robb report — the best pink lady recipe

name: Pontoon Smash
text: pontoon smash ingredients 2 oz bourbon 1 tsp absinthe ¾ oz lemon juice 1 oz pineapple juice ½ oz simple syrup 4 mint leaves 1 mint crown, for garnish steps add all ingredients to a cocktail shaker and shake hard on ice for 6–8 seconds. no need to muddle the mint — the ice will “smash” it for you. fine-strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice. garnish with a mint sprig — and if you're feeling festive, a pineapple slice or leaf. notes batch for 6 (×6): 12 oz bourbon, 1 oz absinthe, 4½ oz lemon juice (about 4 lemons), 6 oz pineapple juice, 3 oz simple syrup, 25 mint leaves, plus 6 mint crowns (one per glass). about 26.5 oz total, plus ice for shaking. source robb report — pontoon smash

name: Rattlesnake
text: rattlesnake ingredients 2 oz rye ½ oz lemon juice ¾ oz simple syrup 1 egg white 1 tsp absinthe (or rinse the glass with absinthe) steps add the rye, lemon, simple syrup, egg white, and absinthe to a shaker and dry shake (no ice). add ice and shake again. strain into a glass (rinsed with absinthe instead, if you prefer). notes rating: b. source bon appétit — the rattlesnake

name: Red Raider
text: red raider ingredients 1¾ oz bourbon ¾ oz lemon juice ½ oz cointreau ½ oz grenadine orange peel, for garnish steps add all ingredients to a cocktail shaker and shake vigorously for six to eight seconds. strain over ice into a rocks glass. garnish with an orange peel (a lemon peel or wheel works too, or skip it). notes use a spicier bourbon at 45% abv or higher, like bulleit or elijah craig small batch. for anything below 45%, bump the bourbon up to 2 oz. reach for a quality grenadine that lists pomegranate, such as liquid alchemist or small hand foods. cointreau is essential here — substitutes like grand marnier don't come out the same. source robb report — the best red raider recipe

name: Revolver
text: revolver ingredients 2 oz bourbon ½ oz coffee liqueur 2 dashes orange bitters 1 large ice cube orange peel, flamed (or regular), for garnish steps add all ingredients to a rocks glass over a large piece of ice and stir. garnish with a flamed orange peel or a regular orange peel. source robb report — the best revolver recipe

name: Sausage Balls
text: sausage balls ingredients 1 lb sausage 1 bar cheddar, shredded 1½ cups flour 1 tsp baking powder ½ tsp baking soda ¼ tsp salt steps preheat the oven to 350°f. mix all ingredients together until evenly combined. roll into 1-inch balls and arrange on a baking sheet. bake for 30 minutes, until golden and cooked through.

name: Scofflaw
text: scofflaw ingredients 2 oz bourbon or rye 1 oz dry vermouth ¼ oz lemon juice 1–2 dashes grenadine 1 dash orange bitters steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice and shake until well-chilled. strain into a chilled cocktail glass. source liquor.com — scofflaw

name: Sentimental Gentleman
text: sentimental gentleman ingredients 2 oz scotch ½ oz bénédictine ½ oz nocino steps stir with ice to chill. strain and serve chilled. source robb report — the best sentimental gentleman recipe

name: Shrimp & Grits
text: shrimp & grits ingredients 2½ cups chicken broth (preferably low-sodium), divided 2 cups whole milk 4 tablespoons butter, divided ¾ cup stone-ground grits 1 cup sharp cheddar cheese, shredded 4+ strips bacon 1 lb jumbo shrimp, peeled and deveined cajun seasoning thinly sliced green onions, for garnish steps cook the bacon. bring 2 cups of the chicken broth, the milk, 3 tablespoons of the butter, and a pinch of salt to a gentle boil. add the grits and whisk together. simmer for 10–15 minutes, until the grits are soft and creamy, whisking every few minutes. cover and set aside. drain all but 2 tablespoons of the bacon grease from the skillet. rinse the shrimp with cold water and pat dry. season with salt and pepper, cajun seasoning, or other spices. cook in the bacon grease until just cooked — about 2 minutes per side. add the remaining tablespoon of butter, ½ cup of chicken broth, and the crushed bacon to the shrimp; stir until the butter is melted and the broth is heated. taste. stir the cheese (1 cup) into the grits until melted. taste. serve the shrimp mixture over the grits, spooning some of the liquid from the skillet over the grits, and sprinkle with sliced green onions. serve immediately. notes didn't like sundried tomatoes. will try diced onion or bell pepper. source bowl of delicious — easy classic shrimp and grits

name: Southern Baptist
text: southern baptist ingredients 2 oz rye ~⅓ oz ginger syrup ½ oz lime juice steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice. shake, then strain into a coupe. notes rating: b.

name: Strawberry Fix
text: strawberry fix ingredients 2 oz bourbon ½ oz lemon juice ¾ oz simple syrup (jalapeño syrup is great) 2–3 strawberries jalapeño half-moon, for garnish steps smash the strawberries in a shaker. add the rest with crushed ice and shake for six to eight seconds. pour unstrained into the glass and garnish with a jalapeño half-moon. notes rating: a with jalapeño syrup. batch: 12 strawberries (mashed), 4 lemons, 16 oz bourbon, 6 oz jalapeño syrup. add crushed ice and shake/stir.

name: Taco Seasoning
text: taco seasoning ingredients 8 tsp chili powder 6 tsp ground cumin 2 tsp paprika 2 tsp crushed red pepper 2 tsp salt 1 tsp garlic powder 1 tsp onion powder 1 tsp dried oregano 1 tsp black pepper steps combine all ingredients and store in an airtight container.

name: The Sazerac
text: the sazerac ingredients 2½ oz rye ¼ oz simple syrup ~5 dashes peychaud's bitters absinthe, to rinse the glass lemon peel, for expressing steps rinse a chilled glass with absinthe, coating the inside, and discard the excess. in a mixing glass with ice, combine the rye, simple syrup, and peychaud's bitters. stir, then strain into the absinthe-rinsed chilled glass. express the lemon peel over the drink last. notes doubled (two drinks): 5 oz rye, ½ oz (3 tsp) simple syrup, 8 dashes peychaud's.

name: Verte Chaud
text: verte chaud ingredients 1 oz green chartreuse 6 oz hot chocolate marshmallows, to top steps make the hot chocolate and pour into a mug. stir in the green chartreuse. top with marshmallows. notes "green hot" — a warm one for cold nights.

name: Wally's Grenadine Old Fashioned
text: wally's grenadine old fashioned ingredients 2 oz bourbon ½ tsp grenadine (instead of simple syrup) 1 dash black walnut bitters 1 dash orange bitters 1 large ice cube orange peel, for garnish steps add the bourbon, grenadine, black walnut bitters, and orange bitters to a rocks glass. add the large ice cube and stir to chill. express an orange peel over the top and drop it in.

name: Wally's House Cosmopolitan
text: wally's house cosmopolitan ingredients 1½ oz tito's vodka 1 oz cointreau or drillaud orange liqueur 1 oz cranberry juice ½ oz fresh lime juice steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice. shake hard for 10–15 seconds. strain into a chilled coupe or martini glass. optional: garnish with a lime wheel or expressed orange peel.

name: Wally's Old Fashioned — Walnut & Orange
text: wally's old fashioned — walnut & orange ingredients 2 oz weller special reserve 1 tsp simple syrup 1 dash black walnut bitters 1 dash orange bitters 1 large ice cube expressed orange peel, dropped in as garnish steps add simple syrup and bitters to a rocks glass. add weller bourbon and stir briefly. add the large ice cube. stir 20–30 seconds to chill and lightly dilute. express orange peel over the drink, rub the rim, and drop the peel into the glass.

name: Ward Eight
text: ward eight ingredients 2 oz rye (or bourbon) ½ oz lemon juice ½ oz orange juice ½ oz homemade grenadine steps add all ingredients to a shaker with ice. shake, then strain into a chilled coupe. notes from the early 1900s — drinks like a cross between a whiskey sour and the red raider.

name: Whiskey Smash
text: whiskey smash ingredients 2 oz bourbon ¾ oz lemon juice (or lime) ½–¾ oz simple syrup 4 mint leaves 1 lemon peel mint sprig, for garnish steps add the bourbon, lemon juice, simple syrup, mint leaves, and lemon peel to a shaker with ice. shake, then strain over fresh ice. garnish with a mint sprig.

name: Whiskey Sour
text: whiskey sour ingredients 2 oz bourbon ¾ oz lemon juice ½ oz simple syrup 1 egg white angostura bitters, for garnish steps add the bourbon, lemon juice, simple syrup, and egg white to a shaker and dry shake (no ice). add ice and shake again, then strain into a glass. garnish with a few drops of angostura bitters on the foam. source liquor.com — whiskey sour

name: WhistlePig S'mores Old Fashioned
text: whistlepig s'mores old fashioned ingredients 1½ oz campstock wheat whiskey (rye is better) ¼ oz whistlepig barrel-aged maple syrup 3 dashes chocolate bitters toasted marshmallow, for garnish steps add the whiskey, maple syrup, and chocolate bitters to a rocks glass with ice. stir to chill. garnish with a toasted marshmallow. notes rating: b. rye works better than the wheat whiskey.
